segments.text: Then Job answered and said,
I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?
Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.
Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.
Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.
Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea.
Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.

footnotes: Arcturus…: Heb. Ash, Cesil, and Cimah

segments.text: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.
Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.
Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?
If God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers do stoop under him.

footnotes: proud…: Heb. helpers of pride, or, strength

segments.text: How much less shall I answer him, and choose out my words to reason with him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, but I would make supplication to my judge.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I had called, and he had answered me; yet would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I justify myself, mine own mouth shall condemn me: if I say, I am perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though I were perfect, yet would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If the scourge slay suddenly, he will laugh at the trial of the innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not, where, and who is he?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away, they see no good.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle that hasteth to the prey.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: swift…: or, ships of Ebeh: Heb. ships of desire

segments.text: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: abhor…: or, make me to be abhorred

segments.text: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, and we should come together in judgment.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, that might lay his hand upon us both.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: any…: Heb. one that should argue

segments.text: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: it is…: Heb. I am not so with myself
